QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_16

question.text: Concentration margin in commodity futures differs from additional/special margin because:

answer.text: It is levied only on select clients with concentrated positions, not on all participants
It applies equally to all market participants
It is lower than regular margin
It only applies to index futures

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Additional and special margins apply to ALL clients in a commodity. Concentration margin targets only specific clients with disproportionately large positions. **The Trap:** Not equal to all participants — that's additional margin. Not lower. Not index-specific. **Cross-Exam Context:** Concentration risk management is specific to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If your position is large relative to the market's open interest, you may face concentration margin — this is the exchange's way of managing the risk from any single large player.
